title: Pan-Seared Fish with Charred Polish Sausage and Gyoza
description: A hearty and flavorful dish that combines the smoky taste of charred polish sausage with the crispiness of pan-seared fish and the savory flavor of gyoza.

ingredients.name: Gyoza Wrappers
ingredients.notes: Available at most Asian grocery stores

instructions: 1. Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add the charred polish sausage and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned and crispy.
3. Remove the sausage from the skillet and set aside.
4. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and cook the fish fillets for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
5. While the fish is cooking, prepare the gyoza by placing a spoonful of your favorite filling in the center of each wrapper.
6. Dip your finger in water and run it along the edges of the wrapper, then fold the wrapper in half to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
7. Add the gyoza to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the bottoms are golden brown.
8. Flip the gyoza over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until crispy and cooked through.
9. Serve the fish and gyoza with the charred polish sausage on top and a drizzle of soy sauce and lime juice.
